posts.title: Ahh Pandemic
posts.text:
Fourth week into my SIParCS internship. Remotely.
Good thing is that I still stay sane (at lease I think so), but for sure I'm missing out a lot by not being able to intern on-site at UCAR in Boulder, CO.
It's getting hot. Oh summer.
We've got a pair of kayaks. We'd go hiking and kayaking around the town. Lake Monroe is awwwwesome.
Just hope the pandemic thing can be over so we can go camping with friends.
And one more thing we learned while practicing the covid-19 social distancing is that we seriously need a dog.

posts.title: First official WFH day
posts.text:
To (efficiently) work in the time of coronavirus is somewhat a tricky thing. 
It's interesting because I found myself more effective and more productive while working from home. 
I get distracted a lot less, and therefore am able to focus on work better.
Or maybe it's only because I just started. 
I have to admit that I was kinda excited to get prepared for stuff -- stocking food and supplies for the following month.
We went to grocery shopping (for the last time!!) this morning. 
I didn't really count our stocks and plan for daily consumption, but hopefully what we have now can last till mid-April. 
I don't know how my friends trapped in China felt when they got to know that they'd be "grounded" for an unlimited period of time, but well, there's some excitement in my preparations. ;p
BTW the cat didn't come to bother me even once. 
I have mixed feelings.

posts.title: Happy New Year 2020
posts.text:
It's already 2020.
I moved to a new lab space on the survey side as the department is moving as a whole. 
I am sitting in this new lab typing my first real blog on this site.
I should probably treat this blog slash personal website more seriously, but, meh.
Some expectations for 2020, as it just starts:

Get more papers written. Or just in general, get more writting done. 
I should probably also revisit the book How to Write a Lot.

Find a better balance between my research and personal life. 
I felt I've done much better in the previous year. 
Thanks to the important persons in my life.

Be happy. In every aspect.


And happy new year to anyone who happens to be reading this post. 
And if it's not within the new year range when you are reading this, have a great day, stranger.
